title: Understanding Bulk Modulus
text: The bulk modulus (B) is a measure of a substance's resistance to uniform compression. It is defined as the ratio of the change in pressure (ΔP) to the relative change in volume $$\frac{\Delta V}{V}$$.

title: Relating Bulk Modulus to Volume Change
text: The formula for bulk modulus is given by: $$B=\frac{-\Delta P}{\frac{\Delta V}{V}}$$. Rearrange this formula to find the change in volume (ΔV).

title: Calculating Change in Volume
text: Given that $$B=2.2\times{10}^9$$ N/m² and $$ΔP=200\times{10}^6$$ N/m², substitute these values into the formula to find $$\frac{\Delta V}{V}$$.

title: Finding the New Volume
text: The initial volume (V) of the sphere can be calculated using the formula for the volume of a sphere: $$V=\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3$$, where $$r=0.2$$ $$m$$.

title: Relating Volume Change to Radius Change
text: The change in volume (ΔV) can be related to the change in radius (Δr) using the derivative of the volume formula: $$ΔV=4\pi r^2 \Delta r$$.

title: Calculate Δr
text: Using the relationship $$ΔV=4\pi r^2 \Delta r$$ and the calculated ΔV, solve for Δr.
